title: CE Chemistry Year 1988 Paper 2 Section MC **Question 39** English version
description:
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

title: CE 化學 | Chemistry Year 1988 Paper 2 Section MC **Question 39** English version
description:
香港中學文憑考試（HKDSE）*化學*科是科學教育學習領域內的甲類選修科目，旨在探討宏觀化學現象與原子、分子和離子等微觀世界之間的連結。學生將培養科學和邏輯思考能力，學會運用化學知識解決問題，並探討化學對社會、經濟和環境的影響。 

DSE 化學科目的主要內容:

**課程設定**:

本課程涵蓋微觀世界、金屬、酸鹼、化石燃料和化學反應等主題。 

**評估**:

評估學生回憶和理解化學知識、使用儀器、進行實驗、分析數據和運用知識的能力。 

**實踐技能**:

本科目強調動手實驗，包括使用儀器、進行實驗和進行精確觀察。 

**問題解決**:

學生將學習分析資訊、解釋數據和解決與化學相關的問題。 

**實際應用**:

本課程探討化學與日常生活、社會和環境的相關性。 

**科學態度**：

鼓勵學生對科學和終身學習建立正面的價值觀和態度。 

本質上，DSE 化學科目旨在為學生打下堅實的化學基礎，培養他們的科學思維能力，為他們進一步學習或在相關領域工作做好準備。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

香港中學文憑考試（HKDSE）*化學*科是科學教育學習領域內的甲類選修科目，旨在探討宏觀化學現象與原子、分子和離子等微觀世界之間的連結。學生將培養科學和邏輯思考能力，學會運用化學知識解決問題，並探討化學對社會、經濟和環境的影響。 

DSE 化學科目的主要內容:

**課程設定**:

本課程涵蓋微觀世界、金屬、酸鹼、化石燃料和化學反應等主題。 

**評估**:

評估學生回憶和理解化學知識、使用儀器、進行實驗、分析數據和運用知識的能力。 

**實踐技能**:

本科目強調動手實驗，包括使用儀器、進行實驗和進行精確觀察。 

**問題解決**:

學生將學習分析資訊、解釋數據和解決與化學相關的問題。 

**實際應用**:

本課程探討化學與日常生活、社會和環境的相關性。 

**科學態度**：

鼓勵學生對科學和終身學習建立正面的價值觀和態度。 

本質上，DSE 化學科目旨在為學生打下堅實的化學基礎，培養他們的科學思維能力，為他們進一步學習或在相關領域工作做好準備。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

本課程探討化學與日常生活、社會和環境的相關性。 

**科學態度**：

鼓勵學生對科學和終身學習建立正面的價值觀和態度。 

本質上，DSE 化學科目旨在為學生打下堅實的化學基礎，培養他們的科學思維能力，為他們進一步學習或在相關領域工作做好準備。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

title: DSE Physics Year 2012 Paper 1A Section A **Question 1** English version
description:
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

title: DSE 物理 | Physics Year 2012 Paper 1A Section A **Question 1** English version
description:
香港中學文憑考試（HKDSE）物理課程旨在培養學生對基礎物理概念的理解、運用這些概念解決問題的能力，以及對物理應用和意義的認識。課程涵蓋廣泛的主題，包括力學、熱物理、波、電磁學以及核子物理。學生還應透過實驗室工作和調查研究培養實踐技能。 

## 香港中學文憑考試物理課程的主要組成部分包括：

- **核心主題** 
 核心課程涵蓋力學、熱物理、波、電磁學和核物理等各個領域的基本物理概念和原理。 
- **選修模組** 
 學生從天文學、原子世界、能源和醫學物理等主題中選擇一個或兩個選修模組。 
- **實務工作** 
 學生參與實驗室工作，包括實驗和調查研究，以培養他們的實踐技能和對科學方法的理解。 
- **評估** 
 香港中學文憑考試物理考試評估學生對課程的知識和理解、他們應用物理概念的能力以及他們的實踐技能。 

## 香港中學文憑考試物理考試的評估目標：

- 回憶和理解物理事實、概念和原則。 
- 運用知識解釋現象和解決問題問題。 
- 了解儀器的使用和實驗方法。 
- 呈現、分析和解釋資料。 
- 清晰、有邏輯地傳達訊息。 
- 了解物理學的應用及其意義。 
- 評估與物理相關的問題。 

## 培養的技能:

透過HKDSE物理課程，學生將培養一系列技能，包括:

- **知識與理解**：打下堅實的物理概念和原理基礎。 
- **問題解決**：運用知識解決物理問題。 
- **實務技能**：進行實驗、進行測量和分析數據。 
- **溝通技巧**：清晰有效地呈現科學訊息。 
- **批判性思考**：評估與物理相關的問題並做出明智的判斷。 

HKDSE物理課程旨在為學生打下堅實的物理基礎和相關技能，為他們進一步學習或從事科學工作做好準備和技術。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

## 香港中學文憑考試物理課程的主要組成部分包括：

- **核心主題** 
 核心課程涵蓋力學、熱物理、波、電磁學和核物理等各個領域的基本物理概念和原理。 
- **選修模組** 
 學生從天文學、原子世界、能源和醫學物理等主題中選擇一個或兩個選修模組。 
- **實務工作** 
 學生參與實驗室工作，包括實驗和調查研究，以培養他們的實踐技能和對科學方法的理解。 
- **評估** 
 香港中學文憑考試物理考試評估學生對課程的知識和理解、他們應用物理概念的能力以及他們的實踐技能。 

## 香港中學文憑考試物理考試的評估目標：

- 回憶和理解物理事實、概念和原則。 
- 運用知識解釋現象和解決問題問題。 
- 了解儀器的使用和實驗方法。 
- 呈現、分析和解釋資料。 
- 清晰、有邏輯地傳達訊息。 
- 了解物理學的應用及其意義。 
- 評估與物理相關的問題。 

## 培養的技能:

透過HKDSE物理課程，學生將培養一系列技能，包括:

- **知識與理解**：打下堅實的物理概念和原理基礎。 
- **問題解決**：運用知識解決物理問題。 
- **實務技能**：進行實驗、進行測量和分析數據。 
- **溝通技巧**：清晰有效地呈現科學訊息。 
- **批判性思考**：評估與物理相關的問題並做出明智的判斷。 

HKDSE物理課程旨在為學生打下堅實的物理基礎和相關技能，為他們進一步學習或從事科學工作做好準備和技術。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

- **實務工作** 
 學生參與實驗室工作，包括實驗和調查研究，以培養他們的實踐技能和對科學方法的理解。 
- **評估** 
 香港中學文憑考試物理考試評估學生對課程的知識和理解、他們應用物理概念的能力以及他們的實踐技能。 

## 香港中學文憑考試物理考試的評估目標：

- 回憶和理解物理事實、概念和原則。 
- 運用知識解釋現象和解決問題問題。 
- 了解儀器的使用和實驗方法。 
- 呈現、分析和解釋資料。 
- 清晰、有邏輯地傳達訊息。 
- 了解物理學的應用及其意義。 
- 評估與物理相關的問題。 

## 培養的技能:

透過HKDSE物理課程，學生將培養一系列技能，包括:

- **知識與理解**：打下堅實的物理概念和原理基礎。 
- **問題解決**：運用知識解決物理問題。 
- **實務技能**：進行實驗、進行測量和分析數據。 
- **溝通技巧**：清晰有效地呈現科學訊息。 
- **批判性思考**：評估與物理相關的問題並做出明智的判斷。 

HKDSE物理課程旨在為學生打下堅實的物理基礎和相關技能，為他們進一步學習或從事科學工作做好準備和技術。 | The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

title: DSE 資訊及通訊科技 | Information and Communication Technology Year sample Paper 1 Section A **Question 1** English version
description:
# 资讯及通讯科技

本科目之公开评核乃根据课程发展议会与香港考试及评核局联合编撰的《课程及评核指引（中四至中六）》《资讯及通讯科技》（**ICT**）进行。考生须参阅该指引，以了解评核所需具备的知识、理解能力、通用技能及实践技能。


# 评核目标

本评核目标旨在评估考生在下列方面之能力：
1. 展示对电脑系统种类及组成，以及硬件、软件和数据之间相互关系之知识及理解；
2. 理解与使用资讯及通讯科技有关之社会、伦理及法律问题；
3. 有效、合乎道德及有辨别力地使用各类应用软件，以支援资讯处理和解决问题；
4. 展示对分析问题、运用资讯及通讯科技规划和实施解决方案之方法之理解；及
5.了解信息素养和利用信息和通信技术共享知识如何影响决策和塑造我们的社会。 | # Information and Communication Technology

The public assessment of this subject is based on the Curriculum and Assessment Guide (Secondary 4─6) Information and Communication Technology (**ICT**) jointly prepared by the Curriculum Development Council and the Hong Kong Examinations and Assessment Authority. Candidates have to refer to the Guide for the knowledge, understanding, generic skills and practical skills they are required to demonstrate in the assessment.


# Assessment Objectives

The objectives of this assessment are to evaluate candidates’ ability to:
1. demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the range and organisation of computer systems, and the interrelationships among hardware, software and data;
2. appreciate the social, ethical and legal issues pertaining to the use of information and communication technologies;
3. use a range of application software effectively, ethically and with discrimination to support information-processing and problem-solving;
4. demonstrate an understanding of methods for analysing problems, and planning and implementing solutions using information and communication technologies; and
5. appreciate how information literacy and the sharing of knowledge using information and communication technologies influence decision-making and shape our society.

title: PastPaper.X | HKDSE PastPaper Flashcard
content:
English version not ava. yet Since we target Hong Kong students, most users has no problem reading both Chinese and English. Multilanguage support is for convenience only, not all content is translated. PastPaper.X 香港DSE 試題(PastPaper) 温習卡 用法１：查找問題和答案 在左上方的搜尋㯗或左方的菜單, 找出你要温習/找答案的題目，打開後畫面如下:
點擊 *解答(卡背)* 可以打開答案 點擊 *問題(卡面)* 可以打開問題(預設已打開) 問題 一般是考卷PDF的*截圖* 答案 可以是局方評卷的*截圖* , 由網上貢獻者提供的文字解答 , Youtube導師詳解等。 *截圖* 指的是本站以JavaScript在數個(和我們無關的)題庫下載的PDF檔案，即時在你的瀏覽器上生成截圖。 本站一直以來都沒有上載或寄存題庫PDF檔案。
你可以在左上方搜尋㯗下的卷源清單
選擇任意一個卷源，它們的內容大致相同。
用法２：温習卡 點擊　設定您的温習計畫
先設定温習範圍 和 一次温習多少題，再點開始學習，系統就會找出不多於這數量的題目。
並自動前往第一題
這樣子進入的題目，在卡背答案下方會出現互動按鈕，讓你標記自己的温習進度。 你更可以靈活輸入延後的時間和難度。
延後的時間 會讓你在指定時間內，*絶對* 不遇上這題目； 當已選出的題目內再沒有可以遇上的題目時便完成了一次温習。 困難度 如果剩餘的題目多於一條，困難的會 *相對* 比較易遇上。 祝願 一起努力學習，拒絶內卷；
先天下之事而事，以助人之樂為樂。 宏揚古賢人之仁，開拓斯同道之道。

title: Chemistry
description:
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

title: Information and Communication Technology
content:
Information and Communication Technology The public assessment of this subject is based on the Curriculum and Assessment Guide (Secondary 4─6) Information and Communication Technology (ICT) jointly prepared by the Curriculum Development Council and the Hong Kong Examinations and Assessment Authority. Candidates have to refer to the Guide for the knowledge, understanding, generic skills and practical skills they are required to demonstrate in the assessment.
Assessment Objectives The objectives of this assessment are to evaluate candidates’ ability to:
demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the range and organisation of computer systems, and the interrelationships among hardware, software and data; appreciate the social, ethical and legal issues pertaining to the use of information and communication technologies; use a range of application software effectively, ethically and with discrimination to support information-processing and problem-solving; demonstrate an understanding of methods for analysing problems, and planning and implementing solutions using information and communication technologies; and appreciate how information literacy and the sharing of knowledge using information and communication technologies influence decision-making and shape our society.

title: Physics
description:
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

title: Chemistry - English version Paper 2
description:
Chemistry - English version Paper 2
Chemistry - English version
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) Chemistry subject is a Category A elective within the Science Education learning area. It focuses on understanding the link between macroscopic chemical phenomena and the microscopic world of atoms, molecules, and ions. Students develop scientific and logical thinking skills, learn to apply chemical knowledge to problem-solving, and explore the social, economic, and environmental impact of chemistry.

Key aspects of the DSE Chemistry subject:

**Curriculum**:

The curriculum covers topics like the microscopic world, metals, acids and bases, fossil fuels, and chemical reactions.

**Assessment**:

Students are assessed on their ability to recall and understand chemical facts, use apparatus, conduct experiments, analyze data, and apply their knowledge.

**Practical Skills**:

The subject emphasizes hands-on laboratory work, including using apparatus, performing experiments, and making accurate observations.

**Problem-Solving**:

Students learn to analyze information, interpret data, and solve problems related to chemistry.

**Real-World Applications**:

The curriculum explores the relevance of chemistry to daily life, society, and the environment.

**Scientific Attitudes**:

Students are encouraged to develop positive values and attitudes towards science and lifelong learning.

In essence, the DSE Chemistry subject aims to equip students with a strong foundation in chemistry, develop their scientific thinking skills, and prepare them for further studies or careers in related fields.

title: Information and Communication Technology - English version Paper 1
description:
Information and Communication Technology - English version Paper 1
Information and Communication Technology - English version
# Information and Communication Technology

The public assessment of this subject is based on the Curriculum and Assessment Guide (Secondary 4─6) Information and Communication Technology (**ICT**) jointly prepared by the Curriculum Development Council and the Hong Kong Examinations and Assessment Authority. Candidates have to refer to the Guide for the knowledge, understanding, generic skills and practical skills they are required to demonstrate in the assessment.


# Assessment Objectives

The objectives of this assessment are to evaluate candidates’ ability to:
1. demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the range and organisation of computer systems, and the interrelationships among hardware, software and data;
2. appreciate the social, ethical and legal issues pertaining to the use of information and communication technologies;
3. use a range of application software effectively, ethically and with discrimination to support information-processing and problem-solving;
4. demonstrate an understanding of methods for analysing problems, and planning and implementing solutions using information and communication technologies; and
5. appreciate how information literacy and the sharing of knowledge using information and communication technologies influence decision-making and shape our society.

title: Physics - English version Paper 1a
description:
Physics - English version Paper 1a
Physics - English version
The Hong Kong Diploma of Secondary Education (HKDSE) **Physics** curriculum aims to develop students' understanding of fundamental physics concepts, their ability to apply these concepts to solve problems, and their awareness of the applications and implications of physics. The curriculum covers a broad range of topics, including mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics. Students are also expected to develop practical skills through laboratory work and investigative studies.

## Key components of the HKDSE Physics curriculum include:

-   **Core Topics:**    
    The core curriculum covers fundamental physics concepts and principles across various areas such as mechanics, thermal physics, waves, electricity and magnetism, and nuclear physics.
-   **Elective Modules:**    
    Students choose one or two elective modules from a selection including astronomy, atomic world, energy, and medical physics.
-   **Practical Work:**    
    Students engage in laboratory work, including experiments and investigative studies, to develop their practical skills and understanding of scientific methodology.
-   **Assessment:**    
    The HKDSE Physics examination assesses students' knowledge and understanding of the curriculum, their ability to apply physics concepts, and their practical skills.

## Assessment objectives of HKDSE Physics examination:

-   Recalling and understanding physics facts, concepts, and principles.
-   Applying knowledge to explain phenomena and solve problems.
-   Understanding the use of apparatus and experimental methods.
-   Presenting, analyzing, and interpreting data.
-   Communicating information clearly and logically.
-   Understanding the applications of physics and its implications.
-   Evaluating physics-related issues.

## Skills Developed:

Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:

-   **Knowledge and Understanding:** Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles.
-   **Problem-Solving:** Applying knowledge to solve physics problems.
-   **Practical Skills:** Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data.
-   **Communication Skills:** Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively.
-   **Critical Thinking:** Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments.

The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

Skills Developed: Through the HKDSE Physics curriculum, students develop a range of skills, including:
Knowledge and Understanding: Acquiring a solid foundation in physics concepts and principles. Problem-Solving: Applying knowledge to solve physics problems. Practical Skills: Conducting experiments, making measurements, and analyzing data. Communication Skills: Presenting scientific information clearly and effectively. Critical Thinking: Evaluating physics-related issues and making informed judgments. The HKDSE Physics curriculum is designed to equip students with a strong foundation in physics and related skills, preparing them for further studies or careers in science and technology.

title: Set up Flash card study plan
description: Begin your study by narrow down you will study through **Subjects**, **Exam** (CE/AL/DSE), **Language**, **Year**, **Paper**, **Section**, **Tags** (Topic), etc.
content:
Begin your study by narrow down you will study through Subjects(required), Exam (CE/AL/DSE), Language, Year, Paper, Section, Tags (Topic), etc.
Also, the amount of card you study per day can be changed ( default is 20 per day).
Each subject can have ONE(1) Study Plan at a time, while Progress (marking a question hard or easy) is stored per unique question and independent from the Plan.
Changing Study Plan will not clean Progress, i.e. Cards/Questions you marked as easy will still appear less often.
(No need to pick all filters, those unchange will be ignored. Since not every combination has results, picking too many filters may found zero related question.)
e.g.
You must select a subject, options related to that subject will be loaded afterward.
Then, you can focus on Tags: Acid and Bases and study all questions reguardless of paper language, year, etc.
Alternative to 2., you can focus on the year 2020, just like having a mock exam.
Playing flashcards are not the only way to use this site, you are free to browse any questions or search for the related answers.
Privacy Statement
This site is made in Hong Kong SAR, China, which do not have cookie law, but we try our best to put your privacy first. We actually maintain a higher standard than most websites.
Your sessional navigation history is stored in Session Storage, which help provide icon on menu bar. These records will not leave your browser and are cleared after you close the browser.
Your study plan and progress will store in Local Storage of your browser. (Different from cookies, data in local storage will not upload to servers.) You have total privacy as this site is made of pre-built static HTML, we don’t even own any server capable of logging.
If you visit through github page or IPFS gateway, those middleware may add some essential cookies which out of our control.
some Questions may contain Youtube(or similiar) Video Answers, and those may contain their cookies.
Advertisements are provided by content providers as a reward for providing content (this site itself is not for profit). Ad platforms will use relevant cookies to analyze which ads are suitable for you. We hope you can support content creators, but if necessary, you can use ad blockers to avoid these cookies.
